Title: An unusual xylan in Arabidopsis primary cell walls is synthesised by GUX3, IRX9L, IRX10L and IRX14

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Primary cell wall glucuronoxylan with pentosylated GlcA side chains (PUX5/PUX6-containing xylan)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues, especially roots and young stems; callus primary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall xylan GlcA side-chain decoration module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream decoration step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly decorates target polymer with GlcA side chains required for the unusual primary-wall xylan structure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid onto the xylan backbone, generating GlcA side chains that can bear an additional pentose in the unusual primary-wall xylan
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GlcA-substituted xylan in primary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for essentially all GlcA substitution on primary cell wall xylan; loss abolishes PUX5/PUX6-type decorated xylan while unsubstituted xylan backbone can still be synthesized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout analysis in young stems/roots; PACE; GH30 digestion fingerprints; MALDI-TOF MS; callus microsome XylT/GuxT assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in Arabidopsis callus; expressed in very young expanding root cells and primary wall-synthesizing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in callus, roots, and young stem primary wall-rich tissues
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray of WT Col-0 callus (Data S1); proteomics dataset from Nikolovski et al. 2012 with 6 peptides detected for GUX3
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young stems, roots, and liquid-grown callus under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for GlcA decoration of primary wall xylan; sole enzyme established for this decoration in the studied tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GUX3 is the only GUX family member required for the GlcA substitutions characteristic of the unusual primary-wall xylan. 2. gux3 mutants lose the PUX5 oligosaccharide in young stems and the GUX3-dependent GH30 fingerprint in roots. 3. gux3 callus microsomes synthesize unsubstituted xylan but lack detectable glucuronyltransferase activity, showing GUX3 supplies the GlcA decorations.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to alter primary cell wall xylan decoration pattern and potentially tune wall cross-linking or biomass properties; effects on gross growth were minimal in the reported mutants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious gross phenotype reported for gux3 single mutant or gux1gux2gux3 under the study conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from GUX1/GUX2, which decorate secondary wall xylan; GUX3-dependent substitutions are regularly spaced every sixth xylosyl residue and are largely unmethylated
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to a flexible, extensible primary wall matrix and modulates interactions/cross-linking with other wall polymers

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Primary cell wall glucuronoxylan with pentosylated GlcA side chains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues, especially roots and young stems; callus primary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall xylan backbone synthesis module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Backbone synthesis, midstream polymer elongation complex
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for synthesis of the xylan backbone that becomes the target primary-wall xylan
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase implicated in xylan backbone synthesis; IRX9 family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required for elongation/synthesis of the xylan backbone in primary wall tissues, likely as part of the xylan synthase machinery rather than proven catalytic transfer alone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Xylan backbone of primary wall glucuronoxylan
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi membrane-associated (inferred from xylan biosynthetic complex context)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss abolishes detectable primary-wall xylan signature PUX5 and eliminates xylosyltransferase activity in callus microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; PACE of young stems; callus microsome XylT assays; expression profiling; promoter swap complementation with pIRX9L:IRX9
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in callus and young expanding root cells; primary wall-specific expression pattern
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in callus, roots, and young stems relative to IRX9
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray of WT Col-0 callus (Data S1); proteomics dataset from Nikolovski et al. 2012 reported ND peptides for IRX9L
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young stem, roots, liquid-grown callus under standard conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required component of the primary wall xylan backbone synthesis machinery
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX9L is strongly expressed in primary wall-synthesizing callus and young root tissues. 2. irx9L mutants lose the primary-wall-specific PUX5 xylan signature and callus microsomes lack detectable xylosyltransferase activity. 3. IRX9 can rescue irx9L when expressed from the IRX9L promoter, indicating tissue-specific expression rather than unique catalytic specialization explains IRX9L requirement.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying primary wall xylan abundance/architecture; promoter engineering may swap function with paralogs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses possible structural/non-catalytic role within a xylan synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports assembly of primary wall xylan that may tune wall extensibility and polymer interactions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IRX9L promoter used for functional complementation with IRX9 cDNA (native-like/tissue-specific)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Primary cell wall glucuronoxylan with pentosylated GlcA side chains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues, especially roots and young stems; callus primary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall xylan backbone synthesis module
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Synthesizes the xylan backbone required for the target polymer
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase implicated in xylan backbone synthesis; IRX10 family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes or is essential for addition of xylosyl residues during xylan backbone elongation in primary wall xylan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss eliminates the primary-wall PUX5 xylan signature and leaves only very low residual xylosyltransferase activity in callus microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; PACE of young stems; callus microsome XylT assays; expression profiling; promoter swap complementation with pIRX10L:IRX10
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in callus and young expanding root cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in callus, roots, and young stems relative to IRX10
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray of WT Col-0 callus (Data S1); proteomics dataset from Nikolovski et al. 2012 with 11 peptides detected for IRX10L
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young stem, roots, liquid-grown callus under standard conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required component; likely principal catalytic xylosyltransferase in this module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX10L is highly expressed in primary wall-synthesizing tissues and detected in callus proteomics. 2. irx10L mutants lose PUX5 and show only minimal residual xylosyltransferase activity in microsomal assays. 3. IRX10 can functionally replace IRX10L when driven by the IRX10L promoter, indicating paralog interchangeability conditioned by expression pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering primary wall xylan backbone synthesis; promoter-based rewiring with IRX10 is feasible
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual activity in irx10L may reflect partial redundancy with IRX10 or weak activity of remaining complex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to primary wall xylan assembly and likely wall mechanics/interactions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IRX10L promoter used for functional complementation with IRX10 cDNA (native-like/tissue-specific)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Primary cell wall glucuronoxylan with pentosylated GlcA side chains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall-rich tissues, especially roots and young stems; callus primary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall xylan backbone synthesis module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Backbone synthesis, accessory/core synthase complex step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for efficient synthesis of the xylan backbone that becomes primary-wall glucuronoxylan
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase implicated in xylan backbone synthesis; IRX14 family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required for xylan backbone synthesis in primary wall tissues, likely as part of the xylan synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Xylan backbone of primary wall glucuronoxylan
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi membrane-associated (inferred from xylan biosynthetic complex context)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss greatly reduces primary-wall PUX5 in young stems and abolishes detectable xylosyltransferase activity in callus microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; PACE of young stems; callus microsome XylT assays; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in callus and young expanding root cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in callus and primary wall-rich tissues; IRX14L low
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray of WT Col-0 callus (Data S1); proteomics dataset from Nikolovski et al. 2012 with 3 peptides detected for IRX14
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Young stem, roots, liquid-grown callus under standard conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required component of the primary wall xylan synthase machinery
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX14 is part of the primary-wall xylan gene expression module with IRX9L and IRX10L. 2. irx14 callus microsomes lack detectable xylosyltransferase activity, showing IRX14 is required for in vitro synthesis. 3. In young stems, PUX5 is reduced rather than absent, suggesting partial redundancy with IRX14L in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter xylan abundance/architecture, though partial redundancy should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Young-stem phenotype suggests IRX14L may partially compensate in planta; discussion notes possible structural rather than direct catalytic role
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports primary wall xylan formation affecting wall organization
